title: Reverse Transcription of Phage RNA
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5rsq1","text":"Reverse transcription (RT) is the process of turning ssDNA into cDNA using the reverse transcriptase enzyme. This protocol uses the RT reaction to turn ssRNA and dsRNA into ssDNA and is specifically used for virome analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"fr3cq","text":"SuperScript IV VILO Master Mix from Invitrogen. Can be bought here: https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/11756500?SID=srch-srp-11756500","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":68,"length":81}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"3B65468185B311EC99D00A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/11756500?SID=srch-srp-11756500"}}}}
steps: Add 16 µL sample to a PCR tube and denature RNA by heating to 95 °C for 00:05:00 on a ThermoCycler
Centrifuge for 00:00:10 and put on ice

Add 4 µL of the 5X master mix to the PCR tube, then run the following program on a ThermoCycler: 25 °C for 00:10:00 (primer annealing) 50 °C for 00:10:00 (reverse transcription) 80 °C for 00:05:00 (enzyme inactivation)
Purify by bead cleaning and elute in 10 µL MQ water
creator: Frej Larsen
